intro: Synotis panduriformis, also known as Chinese Artichoke or Cynanchum, is a perennial plant native to Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Synotis panduriformis include saponins, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Synotis panduriformis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to digestive disorders.
